stem: Which statements about error handling in SamplerV2 are correct? (Select all that apply.)

options.text: By default SamplerV2 applies no measurement-error mitigation; the returned counts are raw samples
Dynamical decoupling is an error-*suppression* technique you can turn on via `options.dynamical_decoupling.enable`
Setting `options.resilience_level` selects the sampler's built-in mitigation strategy
Twirling reduces the number of qubits that get measured
Because the sampler post-processes the data internally, you cannot add your own mitigation

explanation.correct: SamplerV2 returns raw samples with no automatic readout mitigation (A), and its error-handling knobs are error *suppression* (dynamical decoupling) and twirling — DD is enabled through `options.dynamical_decoupling.enable` (B). Everything downstream (readout calibration, post-selection) is left to you.

distractors.C: `resilience_level` is an EstimatorV2 option for expectation-value mitigation; SamplerV2 has no such option, so it cannot select any built-in mitigation.
distractors.D: Twirling inserts randomized Pauli/measurement operations to tailor noise; it does not change how many qubits are measured.
distractors.E: The opposite is true: the sampler returns un-mitigated per-shot data precisely so you *can* apply your own mitigation afterward.
